IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CRIMINAL APPELLATE JURISDICTION
CRIMINAL APPEAL NO.   3126   OF 2026
(@ SPECIAL LEAVE PETITION (CRIMINAL) NO.9415/2026)
RAMESH KUMAR        APPELLANT(S)
VERSUS
STATE OF RAJASTHAN          RESPONDENT(S)
ORDER
1. We have heard learned counsel for the appellant and
learned counsel for the respondent-State of Rajasthan.
2. Leave granted.
3. Appellant is aggrieved by the impugned order dated
11.04.2022 passed by the High Court of Rajasthan at
Jodhpur (briefly the ‘High Court’ hereinafter), rejecting
his application for suspension of sentence during pendency
of the Criminal Appeal before the High Court.
4. It is stated that the appellant has been convicted by
the Court of Additional Sessions Judge, Bhinmal, District-
Jalore,  Rajasthan  in  Sessions  Case  No.22/2016  under
Section 302 of the Indian Penal Code, 1860 (in short
‘IPC’) and sentenced to undergo life imprisonment with
fine of Rs.10,000/-.
1

--- Page 389 ---
5. Appellant’s  appeal  against  conviction  is  pending
before the High Court. Appeal is of the year 2021. In the
meanwhile,  appellant  has  already  undergone  actual
incarceration of more than 10 years. The appeal is not
likely to be heard in the immediate future.
6. That being the position, we are of the view that it
would be in the interest of justice if the sentence is
suspended and the appellant is released on bail during
pendency of the Criminal Appeal before the High Court.
7. Ordered accordingly.
8. Let the appellant be produced before the Trial Court
within  a  maximum  period  of  seven  days  from  today
whereafter the Trial Court shall release the appellant on
bail on such terms and conditions as may be deemed fit and
proper.
9. Accordingly, the Criminal Appeal is disposed of.
10. Pending application(s), if any, shall also stand
disposed of.
…………………………………………………...J.
      
   [UJJAL BHUYAN]
…………………………………………………...J.
     [ATUL S. CHANDURKAR]
NEW DELHI;
13th JULY, 2026.
AK

2. Delay condoned.

issuance of notice to the respondent(s) is considered  
not necessary.
4. This  Miscellaneous  Application  has  been  filed 
seeking the following relief:
“i) Pass orders and direct the Hon’ble  
National  Consumer  Disputes  Redressal 
Commission, New Delhi to prepone and  
1

--- Page 392 ---
decide  the  consumer  complaint  
CC/34/2022  on  merits  within  a  time 
period of two months;”
5. We find that in Civil Appeal No.1434 of 2023  
(Experion Developers Private Ltd. Versus Himanshu Dewan 
& Sonali Dewan & Ors.), this Court vide the judgment  
and order dated 18.08.2023 had set aside the judgment  
and order dated 16.01.2023 pas sed by the National  
Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission (briefly the  
‘Commission’ hereinafter) in Consumer Case No.34/2022  
and remanded the matter for a fresh decision by the  
Commission in terms of the observations and directions  
given in the judgment and order dated 18.08.2023.
6. Learned counsel for the applicant submits that  
almost three years have gone by since then but Consumer 
Case No.34/2022 has not yet been finally heard and  
decided. He therefore prays that some timeline may be  
fixed for disposal of the aforesaid case.
7. After hearing learned counsel for the applicant,  
we  reserve  liberty  to  the  applicant  to  move  an  
application  for  early  hearing  of  Consumer  Case  
No.34/2022  before  the  Commission  and  if  such  an  
application is filed within a period of two weeks from  
today, the Commission shall expedite the hearing of  
Consumer Case No.34/2022 and dispose of the same, as  
expeditiously as possible, and at any rate, within a  
2

--- Page 393 ---
period of three months from the date of first hearing.
8. With  this  observation,  the  Miscellaneous  
Application is disposed of.
9. Pending application(s), if any, shall also stand  
disposed of.

               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Miscellaneous Application No.  1907/2026 in SLP(C) No. 15315/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 29-04-2026  
in SLP(C) No. No. 15315/2026 passed by the Supreme Court of India]
BIRENDRA PRASAD BADONI                             Applicant(s)
                                VERSUS
ATUL BHATT & ORS.                                  Respondent(s)
FOR ADMISSION 
(IA No. 159146/2026 - RECALLING THE COURTS ORDER)
 
Date : 13-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE UJJAL BHUYAN
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ATUL S. CHANDURKAR
For Petitioner(s): Mr. Sukrit Seth, AOR
                   Mr. Saurabh Babulkar, Adv.
                   Mr. Shaurya Pushpam, Adv. 
  
For Respondent(s): Mr. Ratish Kumar Sharma, AOR 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. We have heard learned counsel for the applicant,  
who is the landlord.
2. By  filing  this  Miscellaneous  Application,  
applicant-landlord seeks recall of the order dated  
29.04.2026  passed  by  this  Court  in  Special  Leave 
Petition (Civil) No.15315 of 2026, relevant portion of  
which reads as under:
“2) At this stage learned counsel for the  
petitioners prays for time to vacate the  
suit  premises.  In  the  facts  and  
1

--- Page 395 ---
circumstances, we grant time to vacate the  
suit property up to 31.12.2026, subject to  
payment of arrears of rent within one month  
and regular rent on month to month basis.  
It is directed that the petitioners shall  
hand over the peaceful vacant possession of  
the premises to the respondent on or before  
31.12.2026  and  shall  not  part  with  or  
create third party right therein. A usual  
undertaking shall be filed within a period  
of  three  weeks  from  today  before  the  
Registrar  (Judicial),  High  Court  of  
Uttarakhand. Non-filing of the undertaking  
shall give a cause to the landlord to  
execute  the  decree  and  the  time  for  
vacation as specified above shall not have  
any bearing to the execution proceedings.  
Defiance of the above terms, if any, may be  
treated as non-compliance of the order of  
this Court.
3)  This  order  has  been  passed  without  
issuing notice to other side, however, in  
case  the  respondent-landlord  feels  
aggrieved by the order granting time for  
vacation of the suit premises, he is at  
liberty to apply before this Court.”
3. Though  this  Court  had  given  liberty  to  the  
applicant-landlord to approach this Court if he is  
2

--- Page 396 ---
aggrieved by the order dated 29.04.2026 since the same  
was passed at the admission stage itself and without  
issuing notice, on an overall consideration of all the  
relevant factors, we feel that granting of time to the  
petitioner(s) of Special Leave Petition to vacate the  
suit  premises  by  31.12.2026  is  an  equitable  and  
judicious order.
4. Hence, we do not find any good reason to recall  
the same.
5. However,  we  make  it  clear  that  if  the  
petitioner(s) of Special Leave Petition have not yet  
filed the undertaking before the High Court, as was  
directed by this Court, it would be open to the  
applicant-landlord to initiate execution proceedings.
6. Subject  to  the  above,  the  Miscellaneous  
Application is disposed of.
7. Pending application(s), if any, shall also stand  
disposed of.
(ABHINAV KUMAR)                                (CHETNA BALOONI)
COURT MASTER (SH)                             COURT MASTER (NSH)

ORDER
1. The Transfer Petition is filed by the wife
for transferring the case bearing HMA No. 333/2023,
titled as “Birendra Mohan Mishra Vs. Rashmi Mishra”
from the Court of Principal Judge, Family Court,
Ranchi to the Family Court, District Bengaluru,
Karnataka.
2. The prayer for transfer made in the transfer
petition is not considered, and the petition stands
dismissed accordingly.
3. The  learned  counsel  appearing  for  the
respondent informs the Court that the trial of the
matter has been substantially completed. At this
stage, transfer is not warranted.
4. In response, learned counsel appearing for
the  petitioner  informs  the  Court  that  the
petitioner has to examine two more witnesses, and
the  matter  is  getting  adjourned  for  avoidable
reasons.  In  the  process,  the  petitioner  is
incurring huge expenses.

--- Page 398 ---
2
5. We take note of the submissions of both the
counsel  and,  having  regard  to  what  has  been
recorded  above,  we  direct  the  Trial  Court  to
expeditiously  conclude  the  trial  of  HMA  No.
333/2023, preferably within three months from the
date of receipt of this order.
6. The Trial Court shall make all efforts to
complete the examination and cross-examination of
the witnesses produced by the petitioner on the
appointed date.
7. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand
disposed of.
…………………………………………………...J.
      [S.V.N. BHATTI]
…………………………………………………...J.
           [N.V. ANJARIA]
NEW DELHI;
JULY 13, 2026.

I.A. No.15433/2025-INTERVENTION/IMPLEADMENT
 
Date : 13-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.

         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SHEEL NAGU
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Anup Rattan, Advocate General
                   Mr. Devadatt Kamat, Sr. Adv.

For Respondent(s) :Mr. M C Dhingra, Sr. Adv.
                   Mr. Ashwani Kumar Gupta, Adv.
                   Mr. Gaurav Dhingra, AOR
                   Mr. Shashank Singh, Adv.

                   Mr. Tungesh, AOR
                   Mr. Pravesh Sharma, Adv.
                   Dr. Shipra Tripathi, Adv.
                   Mr. Akhand Pratap Singh, Adv.
                   Mr. Sushil Kumar, Adv.
                                      
                   Mr. Aakash Nandolia, AOR
1

--- Page 410 ---
                   Mr. Saurabh Ahluwalia, Adv.
                   Mr. Abhinav Purohit, Adv.
                   Ms. Ankita, Adv.
                                      
                   Mr. Subhash Chandran K.r, AOR
                   Mr. Anirudh K P, Adv.
                   M/s KRS Associates, Adv.
                                      
                   Mr. Kausar Raza Faridi, AOR
                   Mr. Abhishek Sharma, Adv.
                   Mr. Adv Shahbaaz Jameel, Adv.
                   Mr. Om Tatsat Chaurasia, Adv.
                   Mr. Vikrm A Seth, Adv.
                   
                   Mr. Ritesh Khatri, AOR
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. We are informed by Mr. M. C. Dhingra, learned senior counsel appearing
for the respondent, that not only was she permitted to continue in service till 60
years of age, her retirement benefits have also been released treating 60 years
as the age of her superannuation.
2. In such view of the matter, we are not inclined to keep the special leave
petition pending. The same stands dismissed, making it abundantly clear that
we are not expressing any opinion on merits and the point of law (as to whether
the decision of the State of Himachal Pradesh is legal and valid in rolling back
the age of retirement from 60 to 58 years is correct or not) is left open for
decision in an appropriate case.
3. We further clarify, accepting Mr. Dhingra’s submission, that the State of
Himachal Pradesh shall not deny the respondent any benefit on the premise
that she was supposed to retire at the age of 58 years and not 60 years.
4. At this stage, learned Advocate General for Himachal Pradesh submits
that writ  petitions  are  being  disposed of  by  the  High  Court  relying  on the
judgment impugned in this special leave petition. He prays that an observation
be made that the impugned judgment may not operate as a precedent since
2

--- Page 411 ---
not only writ petitions but other proceedings are pending in large numbers. 
5. Considering such request, it is also observed that the impugned judgment
will not be read as a precedent while deciding pending proceedings. 
5. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
  (RASHMI DHYANI PANT)                       (SUDHIR KUMAR SHARMA)
 ASST. REGISTRAR-CUM-PS                        COURT MASTER (NSH) 
3

IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CRIMINAL ORIGINAL JURISDICTION
TRANSFER PETITION(S)(CRIMINAL)  NO(S).  663/2026
RAJESH SINGH                                  Petitioner(s)
                          VERSUS
THE STATE OF MAHARASHTRA                      Respondent(s)
O R D E R
1. Through this transfer petition, the petitioner seeks
transfer of the Sessions Trial No.565/2023 pending before
the  Additional  Sessions  Judge  -7,  Nagpur  (State  of
Maharashtra) to the competent Court at Jabalpur (State of
Madhya Pradesh).
2. The  case  of  the  petitioner  is  that  offence  was
committed  within  the  State  of  Madhya  Pradesh  and,
therefore, the Court in the State of Maharashtra has no
jurisdiction.
3. It does not appear from the record that any such plea
has been raised before the trial Court.
4. In such circumstances, we decline to entertain this
Transfer Petition at this stage and leave it open to the
petitioner to take the plea of jurisdiction before the
Court where the trial is pending.
5. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed
of.

                    [MANOJ MISRA]
……………………………………………………J
                               [SHREE CHANDRASHEKHAR]

ITEM NO.28               COURT NO.15               SECTION XI
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition for Special Leave to Appeal (C) No. 22756/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 24-02-2026
in WC No. 7352/2026 passed by the High Court of Judicature at
Allahabad]
SURENDRA PAL SINGH & ORS.                          Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF UTTAR PRADESH & ANR.                      Respondent(s)
FOR ADMISSION 
IA No. 189686/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
 
Date : 13-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE UJJAL BHUYAN
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ATUL S. CHANDURKAR
For Petitioner(s) : Mr. Prabhat Kumar, AOR
                    Mr. Abhinav Chaudhary, Adv.
                    Mr. Karan Dang, Adv.
                   
                   
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. We have heard learned counsel for the petitioners.
2. The  High  Court  has  dismissed  the  Writ  Petition  of  the
petitioner assailing the order of respondent No.2 dated 10.07.2024.
While dismissing the Writ Petition, the High Court has relied upon
its earlier decision in the case of Ram Charan vs. State of Uttar
Pradesh.
3. Learned counsel for the petitioners submits that Ram Charan
1

--- Page 421 ---
was wrongly decided; rather it is a judgment in  per incuriam .
However,  on  a  query  by  the  Court,  learned  counsel  for  the
petitioners fairly submits that the Special Leave Petition against
the decision of the High Court in Ram Charan has been rejected by
this Court.
4. That being the position, we are not inclined to re-open a
matter which has already received a  quietus by the High Court.
Accordingly, the Special Leave Petition is dismissed.
5. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of. 
(IRAMNAZ)                                       (CHETNA BALOONI)

[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  06-04-2026
in BA No. 5292/2024 passed by the High Court of Judicature at
Bombay]
MS AMOIN MARIE KOUAME                              Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
INTELLIGENCE OFFICER & ANR.                        Respondent(s)
(IA No. 188559/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING C/C OF THE IMPUGNED
JUDGMENT & IA No. 188561/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.)
 
Date : 13-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE J.B. PARDIWALA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE K. VINOD CHANDRAN
For Petitioner(s) : Mr. Prastut Mahesh Dalvi, AOR
                   
For Respondent(s) : Mr.Siddharth Dharmadhikari, Adv.
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Exemption Applications are allowed.
2. The petitioner, a lady accused and a Nigerian national has
been denied regular bail by the High Court in connection with Crime
No.DRI/MZU/C/INT-157/2023 registered for the offence punishable
under Section 8(C) read with Sections 21(c), 23(c), 28, 29, 30, 35
& 54 of Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances Act, 1985
respectively (for short, “NDPS Act”).
3. We  heard  Mr.  Prastut  Mahesh  Dalvi,  the  learned  counsel
appearing for the petitioner and Mr. Siddharth Dharmadhikari, the
learned counsel appearing for the respondents.
4. We take notice of the fact that the Directorate of Revenue
Intelligent (DRI) is the prescribed agency.

--- Page 435 ---
2
5. Having regard to the nature of the crime and the nature of the
contraband alleged to have recovered from the possession of the
petitioner, we are not inclined to exercise our discretion insofar
the plea of the petitioner for bail is concerned.
6. However, we take notice of the fact that the petitioner is a
31 years old lady and she has two minor children who are being
looked after in Nairobi. 
7. Although the charge came to be framed sometime in December,
2025, yet till this point in time, not a single witness has been
examined.
8. We are informed that prosecution intends to examine in all
sixteen witnesses.
9. In view of what has been stated above, we believe that the
trial court should start with the recording of the oral evidence at
the earliest.
10. Let the trial be completed within a period of six months from
today.
11. With the aforesaid, this petition stands disposed of.
12 Pending applications, if any, also stand disposed of.
 (VISHAL ANAND)                                    (POOJA SHARMA)
DEPUTY REGISTRAR                                 COURT MASTER (NSH)

ITEM NO.3               COURT NO.6               SECTION II-D
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.)  No.8499/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  28-03-2026
in SBCRMFBA No. 12133/2025 passed by the High Court of Judicature
for Rajasthan at Jodhpur]
DALU RAM                                           Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF RAJASTHAN                                 Respondent(s)
(FOR ADMISSION)
 
Date : 13-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE J.B. PARDIWALA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE K. VINOD CHANDRAN
For Petitioner(s) : Mr. Ravindra Bana, AOR                   
For Respondent(s) : 
                   Ms. Nidhi Jaswal, Adv.
                   Mr. Saurabh Rajpal, AOR                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Our Order dated 14-5-2026 reads thus:-
“1. Exemption Application is allowed.
2. Heard Mr. Ravindra Bana, the learned counsel appearing for
the petitioner.
3. The petitioner has been put to trial for the offence
punishable under Sections 147, 148, 149, 302, 307, 394 and 365 of
the Indian Penal Code, 1860 respectively (for short, “IPC”) and
Sections 3 and 25 of the Arms Act respectively.
4. We take notice of the fact that the petitioner is in
custody since 17th August, 2020. 
5. We are further informed that all other co-accused have been
released on bail. However, the fact is that only 15 witnesses have
been examined so far and t he prosecution intends to examine 55
witnesses.
6. Issue notice, returnable on 13-7-2026.

--- Page 439 ---
2
7. In the meantime, the petitioner be released on interim bail
subject to terms and conditions that the Trial Court may deem fit
to impose.”
2. Today, when the matter was taken up for further hearing, the
learned counsel appearing for the State brought to our notice
something  which ought to have been in fact brought to our notice
by the learned counsel appearing for the petitioner.
3. The learned counsel appearing for the State brought to our
notice that the trial has been stayed by the High Court at the
instance of the petitioner since a long period of time. The subject
- matter of challenge before the High Court at the instance of the
petitioner is the Order passed by the Trial Court framing charge
for the offence of murder. According to the petitioner, the Trial
Court could not have framed charge for the offence of murder. 
4. Prima facie, the stand of the petitioner before the High Court
is absolutely unsustainable.
5. We are giving an option to the learned counsel appearing for
the petitioner that if he wants that the interim bail should
continue, subject to further hearing of this matter, he should ask
the petitioner to immediately withdraw the petition which is
pending before the High Court and get the stay of trial vacated.
6. We grant one week time to the petitioner to do the needful.
7. Post this matter for further hearing on 20-7-2026.
8. On the next date, if a statement is not made that the petition
has been withdrawn from the High Court and the stay has been
vacated, we shall ask the petitioner to surrender before the jail
authorities.
 (VISHAL ANAND)                                    (POOJA SHARMA)
DEPUTY REGISTRAR                                 COURT MASTER (NSH)

--- Page 440 ---
1

ITEM NO.44               COURT NO.15               SECTION XI-B
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition for Special Leave to Appeal (C) No. 21153/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 02-02-2026
in RSA No. 45/2026 passed by the High Court of Kerala at Ernakulam]
BABU THOMAS                                        Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
ABDUL BASHEER & ORS.                               Respondent(s)
FOR ADMISSION 
 
Date : 13-07-2026 This petition was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE UJJAL BHUYAN
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ATUL S. CHANDURKAR
For Petitioner(s) : Mr. V. Chitambaresh, Sr. Adv.
                    Mr. Bijo Mathew Joy, AOR
                    Ms. Gifty Marium Joseph, Adv.
                    Mr. C. Govind Venugopal, Adv.
                                       
For Respondent(s) : Mr. Haris Beeran, Adv.
                    Mr. Azhar Assees, Adv.
                    Mr. Anand B. Menon, Adv.
                    Ms. Rizwana R. Raj, Adv.
                    Mr. Radha Shyam Jena, AOR
                   
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. We have heard Mr.  V. Chitambaresh, learned senior counsel for
the petitioner and Mr. Haris Beeran, learned counsel who has
entered appearance on behalf of the respondents by filing caveat.
2. On going through the judgment and order dated 26.09.2025,
passed by the First Appellate Court in Appeal Suit No. 199 of 2017,
more particularly the findings recorded in paragraphs 13 and 14
1

--- Page 443 ---
thereof, we are not inclined to interfere with the concurrent
findings recorded by all the three courts below.
3. At this stage Mr.  V. Chitambaresh submits that atleast two
years’ time may be granted to the petitioner to vacate the suit
premises.
4. We find from the impugned judgment and order dated 02.02.2026,
that six months’ time has been granted by the High Court to the
petitioner for vacating the suit premises.
5. On  due  consideration,  we  extend  the  said  period  till
31.12.2026 by which time petitioner shall vacate the suit premises.
An affidavit in this regard shall be filed by the petitioner before
the Additional Sub Judge, Kottayam in OS No.384 of 2012 within a
period of two weeks from today.
6. Subject to the above, the Special Leave Petition is disposed
of.

[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 24-03-2025
in WA No. 299/2025 24-03-2025 in WA No. 300/2025 passed by the High
Court of Andhra Pradesh at Amravati]
G.L.N. SRAVAN KUMAR                                Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
A. RAMANAMMA & ORS.                                Respondent(s)
IA No. 187273/2026 - CONDONATION OF DELAY IN FILING
IA No. 187272/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING C/C OF THE IMPUGNED 
JUDGMENT
IA No. 187274/2026 - PERMISSION TO FILE ADDITIONAL 
DOCUMENTS/FACTS/ANNEXURES
 
Date : 13-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE UJJAL BHUYAN
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ATUL S. CHANDURKAR
For Petitioner(s) : Mr. Panga Sivanarayana, Adv.
                    Mr. Monu Kumar, Adv.
                    Mr. Ayush Anand, AOR
                                      
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. We have heard learned counsel for the petitioner.
2. Delay condoned.
3. Petitioner  is  aggrieved  by  the  impugned  order  dated
24.03.2025, passed by a Division Bench of the High Court of Andhra
Pradesh  at  Amaravati  (briefly  the  ‘High  Court’  hereinafter)
disposing of Writ Appeal Nos.299 and 300 of 2025.
1

--- Page 445 ---
4. He submits that Writ Petition Nos.6921 and 6928 of 2025 are
pending before a learned single judge of the High Court. When the
matter was adjourned for two weeks for filing of counter affidavit,
the respondents filed the related Writ Appeals. The Division Bench
by the impugned order disposed of the Writ Appeals by directing the
parties to maintain status quo with respect to the land in question
till further orders are passed by the learned single judge in the
Writ Petitions.
5. This order was passed more than a year back on 24.03.2025;
further, the two writ petitions are still pending. At this point of
time, we are of the view that it would meet the ends of justice if
the learned single judge of the High Court is requested to dispose
of the two Writ Petitions being Writ Petition Nos.6921 and 6928 of
2025 within a period of three months from today. Let the petitioner
make a mention before the learned single judge within a period of
seven  days  from  today  by  producing  a  copy  of  this  order,
whereafter, the learned single judge shall do the needful.
6. Accordingly, the Special Leave Petition is disposed of.
7. Pending application(s), if any, shall also stand disposed of. 
(IRAMNAZ)                                       (CHETNA BALOONI)
COURT MASTER (SH)                             COURT MASTER (NSH)
2

         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SANDEEP MEHTA

For Respondent(s) :Mr. Sarthak Nema, AOR
                   Mr. Saket Agrawal, Adv.
                   
        UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
Pursuant  to  order  dated  20.04.2026,  the
petitioner(s) have paid 70,00,000/- (Rupees Seventy
Lakhs Only) to the respondent(s) which is admitted by
the learned counsel for the respondent(s).
Mr.  S.K.  Gangele,  learned  senior  counsel,
submits that the balance 70,00,000/- (Rupees Seventy
1

--- Page 483 ---
Lakhs Only), as per the aforesaid order would also be
paid within the next two months.
On the other hand, learned counsel appearing for
respondent(s)  submits  that  the  amount  of
Rs.1,40,00,000/-,  indicated  in  the  order  dated
20.04.2026, was mentioned without proper mathematical
calculation. However, after due calculation, it has
been found that the actual amount due after receiving
70,00,000/- is Rs.1,07,11,000/- approximately.
We will consider the said objection raised by
the learned counsel for the respondent(s) after the
balance amount of Rs.70,00,000/- as per the order dated
20.04.2026 is paid within the next two months.
List the matter again on 21.09.2026.
By the said date, proof of further payment made
by the petitioner(s) be placed before this Court.
(NEETU KHAJURIA)

For Respondent(s) 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Delay in filing is condoned.
2. Heard learned counsel for the petitioner.
3. We see no reason to interfere with the
impugned order passed by the High Court.
4. The Special Leave Petition is, accordingly,
dismissed.

--- Page 657 ---
2 Diary No. 24859/2026
5. Taking note of the nature of controversy and
the arrangement made by the High Court through the
order impugned, we are of the view that the Trial

DOCUMENTS/FACTS/ANNEXURES)
 
Date : 13-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE S.V.N. BHATTI
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE N.V. ANJARIA
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Abdul Azeem Kalebudde, AOR
                   
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Raghunatha Sethupathy B, AOR
                   Mr. S. Sabari Bala Pandian, Adv.                
                   
                   Ms. Pariksha, AOR
                                      
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. There is a delay of 895 and 376 days in filing the Special
Leave Petitions.
2. For the reasons stated therein and having noted the fact that
the petitioners have availed the remedy of review before the High
Court, we condone the delay.
3. Mr. Abdul Azeem Kalebudde, learned counsel appearing for the

--- Page 667 ---
2
petitioners contends that ‘Shivamma’ respondent No. 1 on 29.04.2015
has executed a release deed in favour of the petitioners who are
purchasers from defendant Nos. 1 to 9.
4. In view of the release deed in favour of the petitioners, the
confirmation of the preliminary decree in the very terms that have
been made by the Trial Court is illegal and unsustainable. He
invites our attention to paragraph ‘20’ of the judgment dated
29.08.2023 and contends that the release deed in fact has been
brought to the notice of the High Court and the finding available
thereon has not been recorded.
5 Issue notice.
6. Adv. Pariksha appears for respondent Nos. 1 to 6 and accepts
notice on their behalf. Hence, notice to the said respondents is
dispensed with. Insofar as other respondents are concerned, the
service of notice be effected within eight weeks through Court and
by post.
7. List on 07.09.2026.
8. In the meantime, there shall be a stay on the passing of the
final decree, and the other proceedings may go on.
    (LOKESH ARORA)                              (NIDHI MATHUR)

                   Mr. Yogesh Vats, Adv.
                   Dr. N. Visakamurthy, AOR                        
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. It has been pointed out in the course of proceedings that 173
witnesses have been listed, out of whom only 18 have been examined
so far. This includes 19 protected witnesses, 4 or 5 of whom have
already been examined. From the remaining protected witnesses, 4
pertain  to  allegations  attributed  to  the  petitioner.  Further
proceedings in the trial are now fixed for tomorrow. With regard to
1

--- Page 701 ---
these circumstances, we direct the prosecution to produce at least
two protected witnesses, who are making allegations pertaining to
the petitioner, tomorrow, i.e., on 15.07.2026.  
2. The Trial Court is directed to ensure that the statement of
all the 4 protected witnesses qua the petitioner are recorded by
31.08.2026.
3. Learned senior counsel for the petitioner assures that no
adjournment shall be sought by the defence counsel for cross-
examination of the witnesses. The Trial Court is also directed not
to adjourn the case at the request of the Public Prosecutor or the
defence counsel and record the statements of the four witnesses as
directed above within the time prescribed.  
4. Post this matter for further consideration on 01.09.2026.
(NITIN TALREJA)                            (PREETHI DILEEP KUMAR)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        DY. REGISTRAR

[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 25-03-2026
in CRMABAU482BNSS No. 1171/2026 passed by the High Court of
Judicature at Allahabad]
BALWANT SINGH                                      Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF UP, & ANR.                                Respondent(s)
FOR ADMISSION 
IA No. 109277/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
IA No. 112118/2026 - PERMISSION TO FILE ADDITIONAL 
DOCUMENTS/FACTS/ANNEXURES
 
Date : 13-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SATISH CHANDRA SHARMA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE NONGMEIKAPAM KOTISWAR SINGH
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Gagan Gupta, Sr. Adv.
                   Mr. Arvind Gupta, AOR
                   Mr. Kanav Bhardwaj, Adv.
                   
                   
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Anurag Dubey, Adv.
                   Ms. Anu Sawhney, Adv.
                   Ms. Maitri Goals, Adv.
                   Mr. Varun Ranjan, Adv.
                   Mr. Ashish Kumar Upadhyay, AOR
                   
                   
                   Mrs. Rachna Gupta, AOR
                   Mr. Anil Kumar Sinha, Adv.
                   
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. This  court  on  20.04.2026,  has  passed  an  order
granting interim protection to the petitioner, directing

--- Page 741 ---
2
the parties to appear before the Registrar (Societies) on
23.04.2026.
2. Learned counsel appearing for the petitioner has
stated before this Court that the order passed by this
Court was not properly explained to the petitioner and he
was under the impression that he has to appear before the
Registrar (Societies) to get the matter settled. In fact,
the intention of this Court was to ensure that mediation
takes place between the parties and the lis is brought to
an end.
3. In  view  of  the  explanation  offered  by  the
petitioner, this court is of the opinion that the interim
order passed by this Court on 20.04.2026 deserves to be
restored and is accordingly restored. However, both the
parties will appear before the Registrar (Societies) for an
amicable settlement of the dispute.
4. The  parties  are  directed  to  appear  before  the
Registrar (Societies) on 27.07.2026.
5. The  petitioner  shall  also  co-operate  with  the
investigation.
6. List the matter in the first week of August, 2026.
  (RAHUL KUMAR)                                    (KOMAL)
COURT MASTER (SH)                             COURT MASTER (NSH)

--- Page 742 ---

ITEM NO.17               COURT NO.3               SECTION II-A
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition(s) for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.)  No(s).  12378/2026
(@ D. No.32443/2026)
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  01-10-2019
in CRLA No. 89/2014 passed by the High Court of Judicature at
Bombay at Aurangabad]
ANKUSH MANOHAR CHAPTE                              Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
THE STATE OF MAHARASHTRA                           Respondent(s)
IA No. 189035/2026 - CONDONATION OF DELAY IN FILING
 
Date : 13-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MRS. JUSTICE B.V. NAGARATHNA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE R. MAHADEVAN
For Petitioner(s) :Ms. Minakshi Vij, AOR
                   Mr.Dinesh Kumar Dagar, Adv.  
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
Delay condoned.
We have heard learned counsel for the petitioner.
We do not find any reason to interfere with the impugned 
order.  The Special Leave Petition is dismissed.
At  this  stage  learned  counsel  for  the  petitioner
submitted that the petitioner is in jail for the last eighteen
years with remission. In the circumstances, the petitioner is at
liberty to seek for remission of his sentence.
It is needless to observe that if an application for remission
is made, the same shall be considered on its own merits, as
expeditiously as possible and in accordance with law.
Pending application(s) shall stand disposed of.

the liability is concerned, the payment of the
amount shall not prejudice the petitioner.” 
2. We  direct  that  the  said  amount,  if  not  already
paid/disbursed, be so done in favour of the claimant(s)
within  a  period  of  two  weeks  from  today.   The
insurer/petitioner shall file an affidavit of compliance to
such an effect, before this Court.
3. We also direct the parties to place on record the
testimonies of the witnesses, and more specifically that of
the owner of the vehicle.
4. List on 06.08.2026.
(RAJNI MUKHI)                                   (ANU BHALLA)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        COURT MASTER (NSH)

[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  23-01-2026
in CRLOP(MD) No. 5796/2025 passed by the High Court of Judicature
at Madras at Madurai]
KESHAV KRISHNA                                     PETITIONER(S)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF TAMIL NADU                                RESPONDENT(S)
FOR ADMISSION 
(IA No. 88913/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.)
 
Date : 13-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SATISH CHANDRA SHARMA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE NONGMEIKAPAM KOTISWAR SINGH
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Renjith B. Marar, Adv.
                   Ms. Lakshmi N. Kaimal, AOR
                   Mr. Arun Poomulli, Adv.
                   Mr. Harsh Vardhan Shyam, Adv.
                   Ms. Jeby Mather, Adv.
                                      
For Respondent(s) :Ms. Vrinda Bhandari, AOR
                   Ms. Vanshita Gupta, Adv.
                                     
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Learned counsel for the petitioner has
stated before this Court that the petitioner
has been in jail for the last 1 year and 9
months and is a young person.

--- Page 791 ---
2
2. Ms.  Vrinda  Bhandari,  learned  counsel
for the respondent-State of Tamil Nadu, has
vehemently opposed the prayer for grant of
bail. She has submitted that the petitioner
has one similar antecedent and that, on an
earlier occasion, 6 kg of ganja was recovered
from him. However, she fairly informed this
Court that he was released on bail in that
case.
3. This Court, after careful consideration
of the documents on record and after hearing
the parties at length, is of the opinion that
the application for grant of bail deserves to
be allowed and is, accordingly, allowed. The
petitioner is directed to be released on bail
forthwith on the usual terms and conditions to
be determined by the concerned Court.
4. The  present  Special  Leave  Petition
shall  stand  disposed  of,  along  with  the

                   Mr. Arjun D Singh, Adv.
                   Ms. Ishika Neogi, Adv.
                   
      UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the
following
                    O R D E R
Leave granted.
The  appellants  stand  convicted  for  the
offence punishable under Section 302 read with
1

--- Page 941 ---
Section 34 of the Indian Penal Code, 1860, and
have been sentenced to undergo imprisonment for
life. The High Court was pleased to dismiss their
appeal against conviction. Aggrieved, the present
appeal has been preferred.
We have been informed by the learned counsel
appearing for the appellants that the appellants
have already undergone incarceration for a period
of five and a half years.
Considering  the  period  of  incarceration
undergone, we are inclined to suspend the sentence
awarded to the appellants, during the pendency of
this appeal.
Accordingly, the sentence of the appellants
is suspended, during the pendency of the appeal,
and they are granted bail on terms and conditions
to the satisfaction of the Trial Court.
Ordered accordingly.
List the appeal in due course.
(ASHA SUNDRIYAL)                  (POONAM VAID)
DEPUTY REGISTRAR               ASSISTANT REGISTRAR

IA No. 79157/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
 
Date : 13-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.

         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANOJ MISRA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE VIJAY BISHNOI
For Petitioner(s) : 
                   Mr. Ram Ekbal Roy, Adv.
                   Ms. Priyanka Das, Adv.
                   Ms. Neha Das, Adv.
                   Mr. Aman Nihal, Adv.
                   Mr. Binay Kumar Das, AOR
                   
                   
For Respondent(s) : 
                   Ms. Tulika Mukherjee, AOR
                   Mr. Beenu Sharma, Adv.
                   Mr. Venkat Narayan, Adv.
                   
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. This Special Leave Petition seeks to impugn judgment
and order of the High Court dated 03.12.2025 by which the
criminal appeal of the petitioner against the order of
conviction has been dismissed.
1

--- Page 975 ---
2. We have perused the record. Upon perusal of the
record, we notice that it was a day-light incident of
murder of which a prompt First Information Report was
lodged and the prosecution case has been substantiated by
eye-witnesses.
3. In such circumstances, we do not find any palpable
error in the view taken by the High Court in affirming the
conviction. Consequently, this Special Leave Petition is
dismissed.
4. However,  dismissal  of  this  petition  shall  not
prejudice  the  right  of  the  petitioner  to  seek  for
remission of sentence as per State policy.
5. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed
of. 
(CHETAN ARORA)                                  (SAPNA BANSAL)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        COURT MASTER (NSH)

               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
SPECIAL LEAVE PETITION (CIVIL) Diary No(s). 7107/2026
[Arising out of impugned judgment and order dated 10-07-2025 in PIL
No. 1/2025 passed by the High Court of Judicature at Bombay]
SAGAR DEVRE S/O. KANILAL DEVRE                     Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
THE UNION OF INDIA & ORS.                          Respondent(s)
(IA No. 89916/2026 - CONDONATION OF DELAY IN FILING, IA No.
89921/2026 - CONDONATION OF DELAY IN REFILING /  CURING THE
DEFECTS, IA No. 89903/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING C/C OF THE
IMPUGNED JUDGMENT, IA No. 89907/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
and  IA  No.  89911/2026  -  PERMISSION  TO  FILE  ADDITIONAL
DOCUMENTS/FACTS/ANNEXURES)
 
Date : 13-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE THE CHIEF JUSTICE
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE JOYMALYA BAGCHI
         HON'BLE MRS. JUSTICE V. MOHANA
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Sanjay Parikh, Sr. Adv.
                   Mr. Raj Awasthi, Adv.
                   Mr. Sagar Devre, Adv.
                   Mr. Satwik Parikh, Adv.
                   Mr. Manish Tomar, Adv.
                   Mr. Chand Qureshi, AOR
                   
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Learned senior counsel for the petitioner during the course of
hearing points out that the High Court, while considering the
judgment of this Court in  M.K. Balakrishnan & Ors.  v.  Union of
India & Ors., W.P. (Civil) No. 230/2001 has not taken notice of the
Office Memorandum dated 08.03.2022 issued by the Ministry of
Environment,  Forest  and  Climate  Change,  Government  of  India.
1

--- Page 1013 ---
Similarly, he relies upon some other documents to urge that there
are other contentions too, which ought to have been considered by
the High Court. In view of the aforesaid, learned senior counsel
seeks and is permitted to withdraw this Special Leave Petition with
liberty to file a review petition before the High Court.
2.  The Special Leave Petition is, accordingly, dismissed as
withdrawn with the aforesaid liberty.

ITEM NO.26               COURT NO.1               SECTION PIL-W
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Writ Petition(s)(Civil)  No(s).  722/2025
B.L. JAIN                                          Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
UNION OF INDIA & ORS.                              Respondent(s)
Date : 13-07-2026 This petition was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE THE CHIEF JUSTICE
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE JOYMALYA BAGCHI
         HON'BLE MRS. JUSTICE V. MOHANA
For Petitioner(s) : Mr. Varun Thakur, Adv.
Mr. Ram Karan, Adv.
Ms. Shardha Saran, Adv.
Mr. Tanuj Bagga, Adv.
Mr. M.K. Ravi, Adv.
M/S. Varun Thakur & Associates, AOR
                   
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The petitioner is a practising advocate. He has filed the
instant Writ Petition under Article 32 of the Constitution of India
purportedly in public interest, seeking a direction to the Union of
India to formulate a National Policy and to draft an Action Plan to
curb  the  watching  of  pornography,  especially  by  minors.  The
petitioner’s case is that the internet has become one of the most
easily  accessible  and  affordable  modes  of  creating,  sharing,
uploading, and viewing pornography. He argues that the excessive
use of pornography causes addiction and psychological distress.  A
lot  of  other  resultant  consequences,  including  commission  of
offences under the penal laws, are also reportedly attributable to
1

--- Page 1017 ---
the watching of pornography. The petitioner further claims that the
Information Technology Act, 2000, does not penalize the watching of
pornography. It merely penalizes the production and distribution of
such material. On this premise, it is also contended that the
authorities are unable to curb the menace of the consumption of
such content.
2. We have heard learned counsel for the petitioner. The issue
raised in the Writ Petition is undoubtedly of paramount public
importance. However, the subject issue is not a question of law
that needs to be determined by this Court. It essentially involves
a  policy  primarily  based  upon  the  use  of  technological
advancements. Such issues ordinarily fall in the domain of experts
and authorities, especially in the Ministry of Information and
Broadcasting.
3. Consequently, without expressing any opinion on the merits of
the case, we dispose of the Writ Petition by granting liberty to
the petitioner to forward a copy of the Writ Petition as a
representation on his behalf to the concerned authorities. We
impress upon the concerned authorities to consider the issues
raised by the petitioner or the suggestions given by him. We have
no reason to doubt that the authorities will give due consideration
to such suggestions.
4. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand closed.
(NITIN TALREJA)                           (PREETHI DILEEP KUMAR)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        DY. REGISTRAR
2

--- Page 1018 ---

Ms. Shrya Smriti, Adv.                          
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The petitioner is aggrieved by an order dated 02.06.2026
passed by the Bar Council of India (BCI) in terms whereof the
election dispute between the petitioner and respondent No.4 has
been referred to a High-Powered Election Supervisory Committee
headed by a former Judge of this Court. The BCI has requested the
said Committee to take cognizance of its order and pass appropriate
orders in respect of the dispute.
1

--- Page 1019 ---
2. In  our  considered  opinion,  this  is  not  the  stage  for
interference by this Court. We deem it appropriate at this stage to
make a request to the High-Powered Election Supervisory Committee
to decide the controversy as early as possible and preferably
within two weeks. The petitioner is permitted to approach the High-
Powered Election Supervisory Committee and seek redressal of his
grievances, including against the order passed by the BCI, so that
he can also be heard in the matter. It is clarified that we have
not expressed any opinion on the merits of the case.
3. The Writ Petition is, accordingly, disposed of with pending
application(s), if any.

Petition for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.)  No.13887/2025
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  25-06-2025

                                VERSUS

Date : 14-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE J.B. PARDIWALA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE K. VINOD CHANDRAN

ITEM NO.26               COURT NO.14               SECTION XVII-A
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
CIVIL APPEAL Diary No(s). 12620/2026
[Arising out of impugned judgment and order dated  23-12-2025 in CC
No. 803/2018 passed by the National Consumers Disputes Redressal
Commission, New Delhi]
M/S ALPHA RADIOS                                   Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY LIMITED                 Respondent(s)
IA No. 175152/2026 – C/DELAY IN FILING, IA No. 175153/2026 –
C/DELAY IN REFILING /  CURING THE DEFECTS
 
Date : 13-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE K.V. VISWANATHAN
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ARUN PALLI
For Petitioner(s)  Mr. Gagan Gupta, Sr. Adv.
                   Mr. Padmesh Mishra, AOR
                   Mr. Varun Maheshwari, Adv.
                   Mr. Vijant, Adv.                   
                   
For Respondent(s)  Ms. Meenakshi Midha, Adv.*
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Learned counsel for the Insurance Company submits that
she has not seen the paper book in the present matter.

[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 02-03-2026
in MCRC No. 2071/2026 passed by the High Court of Chhattisgarh at
Bilaspur]
DHARAM DAS @ DHARAMRAJ YADAV                       Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF CHHATTISGARH                              Respondent(s)
 
Date : 13-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SANJAY KUMAR
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SANJEEV SACHDEVA
For Petitioner(s) :
Ms. Ayushi Pandey, Adv.
                   Mr. Jitendra Kumar, AOR
                   Mr. Santosh Kumar Tiwari, Adv.                 
For Respondent(s) :  
                   Mr. Vinayak Sharma, Standing Counsel, Adv.
                   Mr. Ravinder Kumar Yadav, AOR
                   Mr. Yashvardhan Shah, Adv.                     
         UPON hearing the counsel, the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
Counter affidavit filed by the State of Chhattisgarh discloses
that the petitioner failed to disclose a past criminal case
registered against him.  
In the light of this fact, the law laid down by this Court in
“Munnesh vs. State of Uttar Pradesh”1 applies and the special leave
petition is liable to be dismissed on this short ground.  The same
is, accordingly, dismissed.
Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
(DEEPAK GUGLANI)                             (PREETI SAXENA)
DEPUTY REGISTRAR                         COURT MASTER (NSH)

                                VERSUS

Date : 13-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE DIPANKAR DATTA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SHEEL NAGU
For Petitioner(s) : Mr. K. Parameshwar, Sr. Adv.
                    Ms. Aastha Shrestha, Adv.
                    Mr. Prasad Hegde, Adv.
                    Mr. Sai Kaushal, Adv.
                    Mr. Adit Garg, Adv.
                    Mr. Vallari Km, Adv.
                    Mr. Pushkar Karni Sinha, AOR                   
                   
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Mr.  K.  Parameshwar,  learned  senior  counsel  for  the  writ  petitioner(s),
seeks permission to withdraw the writ petition. He also seeks liberty to pursue
his remedy before the appropriate forum.
2. Permission is granted.
3. The writ petition stands dismissed as withdrawn, with liberty as prayed.
      (MANIK KUMAR)                         (SUDHIR KUMAR SHARMA)
SENIOR PERSONAL ASSISTANT                     COURT MASTER (NSH)

                           [IN CHAMBERS]

          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following

SPECIAL LEAVE PETITION (CRIMINAL) DIARY NO(S). 18710/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 17-11-2022

at Chandigarh]
STATE OF HARYANA                                   Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
SUSHIL KUMAR JINDAL                                Respondent(s)
IA  No.189234/2026-CONDONATION  OF  DELAY  IN  FILING,  IA  No.
189236/2026 - CONDONATION OF DELAY IN REFILING/ CURING THE DEFECTS,
IA No.189235/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
 
Date : 13-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANOJ MISRA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE VIJAY BISHNOI
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Samar Vijay Singh, AOR
                   Ms. Sabarni Som, Adv.
                   Mr. Aman Dev Sharma, Adv.
                   Mr. Gaj Singh, Adv.                             
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Delay in refiling is condoned.
2. This petition is reported to be beyond time by 1135
days.  By the order impugned, the High Court has reversed
the conviction and acquitted the accused on the ground
that the demand of bribe was not proved; the complainant
had been declared hostile and DW-1 had proved that the
accused had advanced loan to the complainant.
3. Having regard to the above, we do not find a good
reason to either condone the delay or to entertain the
special  leave  petition.  Consequently,  the  petition  is
dismissed both on the ground of delay as well as on
merits.
4. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed
of.
  (KAVITA PAHUJA)                               (SAPNA BANSAL)

184165/2026 - CONDONATION OF DELAY IN REFILING /  CURING THE

Date : 13-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.

         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANOJ MISRA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE VIJAY BISHNOI
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Abdul Qadir Abbasi, AOR
                   Mr. Osama Ahmad Abbasi, Adv.
                   Mr. Mohd Azam, Adv.
                   Mr. Arun Chandra Srivastava, Adv.
                   Mr. Imran Ahmad Abbasi, Adv.
                                      
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The learned counsel for the petitioner submits that
the transfer petition pending before the High Court has
since been dismissed vide order dated 03.07.2026.  In such
circumstances, this petition is rendered infructous.
2. In view of the above, this petition is dismissed as
infructous.
   (KAVITA PAHUJA)                              (SAPNA BANSAL)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        COURT MASTER (NSH)

                             O R D E R
None appears for the appellant.

as mentioned in the letter dated 06.08.2023 does not match
with the name of said respondent mentioned in the death
certificate.

the appellant to take steps in this regard.
List the matter after four weeks.
